data.title: FTSE 100 Edges Up Slightly
data.description:
The FTSE 100 edged slightly higher on Friday but lagged behind other European markets, continuing its trend of outperforming in down markets and underperforming when sentiment improves.

This is partly due to its defensive sector weighting and reduced exposure to cyclical macro trends.

This week, oil price swings have been a major influence.

While rising crude prices recently boosted oil giants BP and Shell, today's drop in oil weighed heavily on the index, with both stocks among the biggest losers.

Berkeley Group led declines, down 8%, after reporting a 5.1% fall in annual pretax profits and announcing CFO Richard Stearn as its new CEO. While build costs were stable, weak economic conditions have pressured pricing.

The firm noted improving sales volumes but flagged low consumer confidence.

On the upside, Melrose, Standard Chartered, and Entain gained over 2%.

Meanwhile, UK retail sales fell 2.7% in May, sharply worse than expected, raising concerns about a weakening economy in Q2.

data.title: Beijing Science Sun Subsidiary to Transfer Protein Production Project for 20 Million Yuan
data.description:
Beijing Science Sun Pharmaceutical subsidiary Beijing BGI Protein Research and Development Center will transfer its NeoAB33 new drug project to the parent for 20 million yuan, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The technology transfer entails design, modification, and production of a protein which releases bradykinin and lysine bradykinin from kininogen.

The protein will be helpful in widening blood vessels, increasing blood flow to the brain, kidney, and the heart, while reducing blood thickness.

Shares surged 20% during the close of trading on Friday.

data.title: Zhejiang Kan Specialities Material Unit Gets Approval for NEEQ Delisting
data.description:
Zhejiang Kan Specialities Material said its 67%-owned subsidiary, Kaifeng New Materials, received approval from the National Equities Exchange and Quotations for the termination of its listing, effective June 18.

Kaifeng New Materials will complete post-delisting procedures per regulations, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Shares of the paper products manufacturer closed 1% lower Friday.

data.title: Poonawalla Fincorp Says Issuing NCDs Worth Up To 16 Bln Rupees
data.description:
Poonawalla Fincorp Ltd :

  • ISSUING NCDS WORTH UP TO 16 BILLION RUPEES

data.title: DB cuts Wacker Chemie to 'hold' on short-term earnings risks
data.description:
** Deutsche Bank downgrades Wacker Chemie to "hold" from "buy", citing increased short-term earnings risks, especially in the German chemical maker's solar division

** The downgrade reflects forex, softer solar demand, and a weaker macro environment, Deutsche Bank analyst Tristan Lamotte says

** Wacker faces challenges in its solar and polysilicon business, including reduced tax credits, supply chain reshuffling, and possible capacity additions, the broker says

** Deutsche Bank lowers its estimates for Q2 and FY 2025, as well as for 2026, adding that all three are now below consensus

** Wacker Chemie's stock is the worst performer on Germany's mid-cap index , down 0.8%

data.title: The CAC 40 Index Opens 0.69% Higher
data.description:
In Paris, the CAC 40 Index is gaining 52 points or 0.69 percent on Friday.

Leading the gains are Kering (3.13%), STMicroelectronics (2.24%) and Societe Generale (1.79%).

Top losers are TotalEnergies SE (-0.64%), Teleperformance SE (-0.56%) and Veolia (-0.46%).

data.title: Sai Life Sciences Has Begun Commercial Operations of Second Phase of  Bidar, India Unit Expansion
data.description:
Sai Life Sciences has begun commercial operations of the second phase of the production block with 91 kiloliter of capacity at its Bidar facility in Karnataka, India.

This is the second and final phase of the unit's overall capacity addition of 195 kiloliter, according to a Friday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

With this, the combined capacity of Unit IV Bidar facility will be 640 kiloliter, which will supply to registered starting materials (RSM), intermediates, and active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) for clinical and commercial purposes.

The company's shares were up nearly 1% in recent trade.

data.title: UK's Berkeley falls after chairman steps down
data.description:
** Shares of British high-end homebuilder Berkeley fall 8.9% to 3,780p; stock among top losers on FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index and on track for biggest pct drop since March 2020

** Co names Rob Perrins as new executive chair; Michael Dobson to step down on September 5

** Shares of co have risen 20.4% during Dobson's near three-year tenure

** Co also appoints Richard Stearn as new CEO

** Reaffirms pre-tax profit guidance of FY26 at 450 million pounds ($606.2 million) vs analysts' estimates of 477 million pounds - data compiled by LSEG

** Plans to invest 5 billion pounds for next 10 years, which will deliver 4,000 additional homes

** YTD, stock up 6.41%

($1 = 0.7424 pounds)

data.title: PRESSR: Archer partners with Jetex to integrate global infrastructure portfolio into Air Taxi Network
data.description:
• The companies will work together to design the passenger experience, deploy charging infrastructure and integrate Archer’s Midnight eVTOL aircraft into daily operations across Jetex's international portfolio of 40 private aircraft terminals in more than 30 countries.
  • This strategic alliance builds on Archer's growing infrastructure network and partnerships with other leaders in the industry, establishing a vertiport network that can support the growth of Archer’s air taxi operations globally.

Paris, France – Archer today announced a strategic partnership with Jetex, a leading global aviation services provider, focused on developing infrastructure across Jetex’s international portfolio of private terminals to support Archer’s planned commercial air taxi network.

This collaboration, planned to focus initially on infrastructure in the United Arab Emirates, is expected to expand to additional sites around the world across Jetex’s global portfolio, which includes 40 private aircraft terminals in more than 30 countries.

Building upon Archer’s prior partnerships with other leading infrastructure providers, this approach advances Archer’s plans to scale a vertiport network that can support the growth plans for Archer’s air taxi operations globally.

Under this partnership, the two companies plan to enhance Jetex's strategically located fixed base operator (FBOs) locations to better suit Archer’s Midnight aircraft. Archer plans to work with Jetex to design the passenger experience and integrate eVTOL aircraft into daily operations.These upgrades will be paired with Jetex's luxury service standards to deliver an efficient, tech-enabled customer journey.

Adel Mardini, Founder & CEO of Jetex commented: “eVTOL technology is an instrumental part of our industry, designed to transform urban mobility and shape the future of business aviation. We are proud to work with our partners at Archer on making this ambitious vision a reality at Jetex Abu Dhabi and our other global destinations.”

Adam Goldstein, founder and CEO of Archer, said, “Building a beautiful, safe and high-performing aircraft is only one aspect of our business. To succeed in the UAE and beyond we need to pair Midnight with strategic infrastructure that allows us to operate by delivering a premium experience for our passengers. With their wide-ranging global portfolio, and deep expertise with high-end customer service, Jetex is a perfect fit as a partner in Archer’s commercialization strategy.”

The announcement comes as Archer continues to advance its certification and commercialization efforts in the UAE and beyond, building on recent achievements including regulatory design approval for the UAE's first hybrid heliport at Abu Dhabi Cruise Terminal and ongoing partnerships with leading regional operators including Abu Dhabi Aviation.

About Archer

Archer is designing and developing the key enabling technologies and aircraft necessary to power the future of aviation. 

About Jetex

An award-winning global leader in private aviation, Jetex is recognized for delivering flexible, best-in-class solutions to customers worldwide. Jetex provides exceptional private terminals (FBOs), lifestyle concierge and aircraft charter services, as well as fuelling, ground handling and global trip planning. The company caters to both owners and operators of business jets for corporate, commercial and personal air travel.

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

data.title: First Abu Dhabi Bank Joins China's Cross-border RMB System as Direct Participant
data.description:
First Abu Dhabi Bank (ADX:FAB) said Friday it is now a direct participant in the Cross-border Interbank Payment System, the official payment infrastructure for cross-border renminbi transactions.

The move bolsters the Abu Dhabi-based lender's capabilities in providing RMB clearing and settlement services, further strengthening its position in cash management across the Middle East and North Africa.

First Abu Dhabi Bank is the first MENA-based bank to join CIPS as a direct participant.

data.title: Russia's Sberbank CEO says rouble is overvalued, should be at 100-plus to the dollar
data.description:
Key points:
  • High key rate behind rouble strength
  • Strong rouble hurts exporters, budget, Sberbank CEO says
  • Deputy PM Novak says time to cut rates

The Russian rouble is overvalued and this is hitting the revenues of exporters and the state budget, German Gref, CEO of Russia's largest lender Sberbank, said on Friday, suggesting that a rate of over 100 to the dollar would be appropriate.

The Russian currency has strengthened by over 40% against the dollar since the start of the year and was trading around 78.20 to the dollar on Friday.

"The exchange rate is far from its equilibrium. It is obvious that an equilibrium exchange rate is 100-plus to the U.S. dollar," Gref said at Russia's showcase economic forum in St Petersburg.

Analysts cite high interest rates, low demand for imports and hopes for an easing of geopolitical tensions over Russia's military action in Ukraine as the main factors behind the rouble rally.

Business leaders say that Russia's bank deposit rates at over 20% prevent investment because it is more profitable to keep money in a bank.

Gref said the suspension of investment was a threat to economic growth "not only this year, but also on the horizon of two to three years".

For exporters, including Russia's energy majors, and the budget, Gref said the overvalued rouble had shrunk revenues.

"We see that the budget is facing an increased deficit. At the end of the year the Finance Ministry will have to cover this deficit from some new sources," Gref said. He said raising money on the market was an option.

The central bank hiked its benchmark interest rate to 21% last year to fight inflation.

It cut the rate by one percentage point to 20% earlier this month, saying inflation was slowing.

Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak said that the economy had the potential to grow at a rate of 3%.

"All indicators are showing that it is time to cut the rate and start heating up the economy," Novak said.

data.title: Moldova Producer Inflation Slows to 5% in May
data.description:
Producer prices in Moldova rose 5% year-on-year in May 2025, slowing from a 5.2% increase in the previous month.

Producer inflation edged down for mining and quarrying (12.7% vs 12.9% in April), and manufacturing (2.8% vs 2.5%).

Meanwhile, prices for electricity, gas, steam, and air-conditioning supply held steady at 25.4%.

On a monthly basis, producer prices climbed 1.6%, following a 0.5% increase in April.

data.title: Amundi Has Strong Growth Outlook Supported by Third-Party Distribution — Market Talk
data.description: Amundi's third-party distribution client segment is a key growth pillar, Citi says in a research note. The French asset manager updated its outlook for the segment, which is taking share in a growing market. It has achieved a 21% compound annual growth rate for its assets under management over the 2020-24 period, analysts write. The business has around 600 distribution contracts and is already well-diversified by geography and client type. The demand from distributors for players that offer breadth and efficient scaling benefits incumbents like Amundi. "We came away reassured by the strength and breadth of Amundi's offering, its strong market positioning, as well as its long-term growth trajectory, supported by attractive M&A optionality," Citi says. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Gilt Yields Fall After Weaker-Than-Forecast Retail Sales — Market Talk
data.description: Yields on U.K. government bonds fall following weaker-than-forecast retail sales data. The monthly retail sales contracted 2.7% in May, weaker than the consensus forecast of 0.6% contraction by economists in a WSJ poll. The data raises the possibility of the Bank of England cutting interest rates in August, Omnis Investments' Patrick O'Donnell says in a note. The 10-year gilt yields fall around 3 basis points to last trade at 4.501%, Tradeweb data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Top Reasons Why Altcoin Season 2025 Is Far From Sight
data.description:
With Bitcoin hovering well above $100k and altcoins failing at cycle lows, investors are curious if the altcoin season 2025, is still on the cards, or have we missed the boat? It is rightly said that history shows a rhythm in market behavior, while many claim “this cycle is different,” the data says otherwise. 

From BTC dominance and institutional inflows to the Altcoin Season Index hitting rock bottom, all signs point to a forthcoming shift in liquidity. In this analysis I take you through crypto analyst Tracer’s thesis on altseason.

Bitcoin Is Soaring, But Alts Are Sleeping?

It has been disappointing to the marketers that, despite the total market cap sitting at $3.24 trillion, altcoins are still significantly underperforming relative to Bitcoin. Data reveals that most alts remain down 80–90% from their all-time highs. This is all while BTC continues breaking new streaks. The analyst highlights that this discrepancy isn’t new, as in past cycles, altseason only kicked off after Bitcoin concreted its top.

The Altcoin Season Index currently sits at 21/100, deeply into “Bitcoin Season” territory. Historically, these levels have marked the start to capital rotation from BTC into alts once Bitcoin shows signs of topping out.

Institutions Are Still Going All-In on Bitcoin?

Institutional behavior has tilted heavily toward Bitcoin. From Strategy’s billion-dollar buys to publicly traded firms and sovereign institutions building BTC treasuries. 

Coming to the M2 global money supply, a key liquidity driver, shows a strong correlation with BTC, especially with a 76-day offset. As M2 expands, Bitcoin absorbs the initial liquidity. But when this flow saturates, past actions suggest altcoins often catch the spillover, triggering the true altseason.

Key Market Signals Flashing the Setup

The following market indicators are pointing to a classic pre-altseason setup:

  • Bitcoin Dominance: Currently hovering at record highs. Past patterns show that when BTC.D drops below 55%, it’s a clear sign that capital is rotating into alts.
  • Trading Volume Divergence: BTC’s volumes have recently dipped compared to previous bull cycles from 2016 and 2021, and alt volumes remain sluggish. A simultaneous volume spike in alts, along with a BTC.D drop, will be a major trigger.
  • Fear & Greed Index: Sitting neutral at 48, indicating indecision, a common trait to market transitions.

Also worth noting is the geopolitical tension with Iran, which has temporarily frozen liquidity. However, such geopolitical dips tend to resolve swiftly and provide golden buying opportunities.

Keen on BTC’s future? Read our Bitcoin Price Prediction 2025, 2026-2030!

FAQs

When will the altcoin season start?

The altcoin season 2025 will start once the Bitcoin dominance drops below 55, volume flows into alts, and the altcoin season index rises above 75.

Should I be buying alts now or wait?

The current metrics suggest we’re in a prime accumulation phase. History favors those who enter before the crowd, not during euphoric runs.

data.title: New World Could Attract Competing Bid After CAML Takeover Offer, Euroz Says
data.description:
New World Resources may attract a competing takeover bid following a sweetened offer from UK-based mining company Central Asia Metals (CAML), according to a Friday note from Euroz Hartleys.

In a same-day filing, the company said CAML raised its offer to AU$0.053 per share from AU$0.05 and agreed to provide the company with interim funding of AU$10 million via the issue of around 188.7 million shares at the same price as the improved takeover offer.

However, the company's 11.99% stakeholder Kinterra Capital on Friday applied to the Australian Government Takeover Panel, alleging that a proposed share placement by NWC could frustrate any potential competing bid for the company.

Kinterra sought interim orders to prevent the placement from proceeding before the panel hears the case and final orders from the panel to block the placement, arguing it may deter alternative proposals.

Euroz has a speculative buy rating on the company.

Shares of the company rose 8% at market close.

data.title: Unison Shares Plunge Nearly 21% After Stock Issuance Announcement
data.description:
Shares of South Korean wind power firm Unison (KOSDAQ:018000) plunged 20.9% at market close on Friday, a day after the company announced its plans to raise 64.3 billion won via shares issuance of 51 million common shares.

The shares will be issued at 1,260 won per share, the company said in a regulatory filing with the Korea Exchange on Thursday.

data.title: NIIF Infra Finance to issue multiple tenor bonds, bankers say
data.description:
India's NIIF Infrastructure Finance plans to raise 6.50 billion rupees ($75.1 million), including a greenshoe option of 2.25 billion rupees, through the sale of over three-year, over five-year and reissue of 8.07% 2039 bonds, three bankers said on Friday.

The company has invited bids from bankers and investors on Monday.

It did not reply to a Reuters email seeking comment.

data.title: European shares rebound as worries of US involvement in Middle East ease
data.description:
European shares rebounded on Friday after three sessions of declines, as a stall in the United States' involvement in the Middle East conflict came as a relief to worried investors.

The pan-European STOXX 600 was up 0.4% at 537.98 points at 0708 GMT. The benchmark is on track to log weekly declines for a second week.

Israel and Iran's air war entered a second week and European officials sought to draw Tehran back to the negotiating table after President Donald Trump said any decision on potential U.S. involvement would be made within two weeks.

The news improved market mood and helped recover some interest in risk assets that were being sold off during the week on uncertainty around how long the conflict would go on.

In the market, travel and leisure stocks (.SXTP) gained the most, up 1.1%, as oil prices eased.

Conversely, energy shares (.SXEP) were at the bottom of the index with a 0.7% decline, trimming some gains from this week.

Among stocks, London's Berkeley was the biggest percentage decliner, down 8%. The homebuilder named current finance chief Richard Stern as its new CEO, but reported an annual pre-tax profit slightly ahead of market expectations.

data.title: Merchant House International Completes Asset Sales at Closed US Textile Factory
data.description:
Merchant House International said following extensive negotiation with potential purchasers, it has sold the factory and land at its now-closed American Merchant textile factory to Leclerc Foods Virginia for net proceeds of $8 million, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The company has received full settlement funds, per the filing.

The book value of the factory and land at March 31 was AU$8.8 million and based on sale proceeds of about AU$12.3 million, a profit of about AU$3.5 million will be realised from the sale, the filing added.

The company also sold a condominium with a book value of AU$74,667 for AU$150,641, realising a profit of AU$75,974.

The company has received full payment of $4 million from a previously disclosed sale of equipment from the factory.

The transactions conclude the sale of all of the company's assets related to the factory.

data.title: IIFL Finance to issue multiple tenor bonds, bankers say
data.description:
India's IIFL Finance plans to raise 6 billion rupees ($69.30 million), including a greenshoe option of 4.50 billion rupees, through the sale of bonds maturing in seven years and 10 years, three bankers said on Friday.

The company has invited bids from bankers and investors on Monday, they said.

IIFL Finance did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.

data.title: Rome Resources Says Rwanda, Congo Peace Deal Good for Business
data.description:
Rome Resources said Friday that a planned peace deal between Rwanda and the Democratic Republic of the Congo would help boost the business environment, particularly in the eastern region of Congo.

The tin and base metals explorer noted a report published by British news outlet BBC, which stated that the two countries plan to sign a definitive peace agreement during the week of June 23.

Rome Resources uses the Bisie airstrip in the Congo to support its three nearby drill rigs.

data.title: Chemify Launches the World's First Chemputation Facility Revolutionizing Molecular Design and Manufacture
data.description:
Glasgow, Scotland--(Newsfile Corp. - June 20, 2025) - Chemify, the deep-tech pioneer fusing chemistry, robotics, computation and AI to digitise molecule creation, today unveiled its first Chemifarm--a fully automated chemistry facility regarded as the world's most advanced laboratory for molecular design and synthesis.

To view the full announcement, including downloadable images, bios, and more, click here.

Key Takeaways:

  • Chemify's first Chemifarm is a fully automated chemistry facility regarded as the world's most advanced laboratory for molecular design and synthesis
  • Chemify's AI-driven molecular-design engine with industrial-scale robotic synthesis accelerates the discovery of small-molecule medicines and next-generation materials for partners tackling society's toughest challenges
  • The lb12 million project is supported through the Glasgow City Region Innovation Accelerator Programme, a UK Government-led initiative delivered by Innovate UK. Scottish Enterprise also provided a grant of lb1.1M to Chemify for the project. Chemify's new facility will create 60 new jobs and safeguard over 50 existing roles in Glasgow, Scotland

Click image above to view full announcement.

About Chemify

Chemify is reimagining the discovery and synthesis of novel molecules for medicines and advanced materials by turning digital code into physical compounds through Chemputation--its fusion of robotics and AI. Headquartered in Glasgow, Scotland, the company was founded by Lee Cronin to digitalise chemistry at global scale for the benefit of humanity. Learn more at Chemify.io and follow @ChemifyX on X/Twitter. 

Contacts:

Michelle Linn

michelle@linndencom.com

Source: Chemify

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/256231

data.title: PolarX Secures $2 Million Loan From Northern Star Resources
data.description:
PolarX largest shareholder, Northern Star Resources (ASX: NST), has agreed to lend the company $2 million to fund resource expansion at its Alaska Range Project in the US, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The 12-month loan is unsecured and interest-free for the first three months and 7% thereafter, with $2 million fully drawn on inception.

The company said the funds allow it to immediately mobilize an exploration team and equipment for the upcoming work program at its high-grade Caribou Dome Copper Project within the Alaska Range in the US.

The work program includes an extensive geophysics program and drilling campaign, which will run throughout the coming field season, the filing added.
